courseName: Human Anatomy and Physiology II - Theory
scope: This subject is designed to impart a fundamental knowledge on the structure and functions of the various systems of the human body. It also helps in understanding both homeostatic mechanisms and cellular pathways under standard therapeutic regimens.
objectives: Describe the structure, location and basic physiology of various organs of the human body
Comprehend the homeostatic mechanisms and energetic pathways of the body
Appreciate the coordinated working pattern of different organs of each system
Appreciate the concepts of genetics and introduction to human chromosomes
courseOutcomes: CO1: Understand and explain the organization and function of the central and autonomic nervous systems.
CO2: Describe the anatomy and physiology of the digestive system and evaluate cellular bioenergetics.
CO3: Explain the anatomy and physiological mechanisms of respiration and renal excretion.
CO4: Discuss the endocrine glands, their secretions, and the hormonal control systems.
CO5: Describe the reproductive systems, physiology of menstruation, pregnancy, and basic genetics.

topics.topicName: Nervous system: Organization of nervous system, neuron, neuroglia, classification and properties of nerve fibre, electrophysiology, action potential, nerve impulse conduction, receptors, synapse, neurotransmitters.
topics.name: Nervous system: Organization of nervous system, neuron, neuroglia, classification and properties of nerve fibre, electrophysiology, action potential, nerve impulse conduction, receptors, synapse, neurotransmitters.

topics.topicName: Central nervous system: Meninges, ventricles of brain and cerebrospinal fluid. Structure and functions of brain (cerebrum, brain stem, cerebellum), spinal cord (gross anatomy, sensory and motor tracts, reflex activity).
topics.name: Central nervous system: Meninges, ventricles of brain and cerebrospinal fluid. Structure and functions of brain (cerebrum, brain stem, cerebellum), spinal cord (gross anatomy, sensory and motor tracts, reflex activity).

topics.topicName: Digestive system: Anatomy of GI Tract with special reference to anatomy and functions of stomach, acid production in the stomach, regulation of acid production, small intestine and large intestine, anatomy and functions of salivary glands, liver and pancreas, digestion and absorption of food.
topics.name: Digestive system: Anatomy of GI Tract with special reference to anatomy and functions of stomach, acid production in the stomach, regulation of acid production, small intestine and large intestine, anatomy and functions of salivary glands, liver and pancreas, digestion and absorption of food.

topics.topicName: Energetics: Formation and role of ATP, Creatinine Phosphate and BMR.
topics.name: Energetics: Formation and role of ATP, Creatinine Phosphate and BMR.

topics.topicName: Respiratory system: Anatomy of respiratory organs; Mechanism of respiration, regulation of respiration, transport of respiratory gases, pulmonary ventilation volumes and capacities.
topics.name: Respiratory system: Anatomy of respiratory organs; Mechanism of respiration, regulation of respiration, transport of respiratory gases, pulmonary ventilation volumes and capacities.

topics.topicName: Urinary system: Anatomy of urinary tract with special reference to anatomy of kidney and nephron, physiology of urine formation, micturition reflex and role of kidneys in acid base balance.
topics.name: Urinary system: Anatomy of urinary tract with special reference to anatomy of kidney and nephron, physiology of urine formation, micturition reflex and role of kidneys in acid base balance.

topics.topicName: Endocrine glands: Pituitary gland, thyroid gland, parathyroid gland, adrenal gland, pancreas, pineal gland, thymus and their hormones.
topics.name: Endocrine glands: Pituitary gland, thyroid gland, parathyroid gland, adrenal gland, pancreas, pineal gland, thymus and their hormones.

topics.topicName: Hormonal control: Classification of hormones, mechanism of hormone action, endocrine disorders (hypo and hyper secretions).
topics.name: Hormonal control: Classification of hormones, mechanism of hormone action, endocrine disorders (hypo and hyper secretions).

topics.topicName: Reproductive system: Anatomy of male and female reproductive organs, physiology of reproduction, spermatogenesis, oogenesis, pregnancy and parturition, menstrual cycle, contraceptive devices.
topics.name: Reproductive system: Anatomy of male and female reproductive organs, physiology of reproduction, spermatogenesis, oogenesis, pregnancy and parturition, menstrual cycle, contraceptive devices.

topics.topicName: Introduction to genetics: Chromosomes, genes, DNA replication, transcription, translation, cell division (mitosis and meiosis), gene expression, genetic disorders.
topics.name: Introduction to genetics: Chromosomes, genes, DNA replication, transcription, translation, cell division (mitosis and meiosis), gene expression, genetic disorders.

scope: This subject deals with the classification, nomenclature, isomerism, and properties of organic compounds. The syllabus emphasizes the mechanisms and orientation of reactions.
objectives: Write the structure, name and the type of isomerism of the organic compound
Write the reaction, name the reaction and orientation of reactions
Account for reactivity/stability of compounds
Identify the organic compounds by qualitative and quantitative tests
courseOutcomes: CO1: Classify organic compounds, write their IUPAC names, and identify structural isomerism.
CO2: Describe preparation and reactions of alkanes, alkenes, and conjugated dienes.
CO3: Explain nucleophilic substitution mechanisms (SN1, SN2) of alkyl halides and properties of alcohols.
CO4: Understand carbonyl compounds (aldehydes, ketones) and their nucleophilic addition reactions.
CO5: Explain properties, synthesis, acidity, and basicity of carboxylic acids and aliphatic amines.

topics.topicName: Classification of Organic Compounds, Common and IUPAC systems of nomenclature of organic compounds (Aliphatic, Alicyclic and Aromatic compounds).
topics.name: Classification of Organic Compounds, Common and IUPAC systems of nomenclature of organic compounds (Aliphatic, Alicyclic and Aromatic compounds).

topics.topicName: Structural isomerism in organic compounds.
topics.name: Structural isomerism in organic compounds.

topics.topicName: Alkanes*: Halogenation of alkanes, uses of paraffin, free radical mechanisms.
topics.name: Alkanes*: Halogenation of alkanes, uses of paraffin, free radical mechanisms.

topics.topicName: Alkenes*: E1 and E2 reactions, kinetics, order of reactivity of alkyl halides, rearrangement of carbocations, Saytzeffs orientation, free radical addition of HBr (anti-Markovnikov's), qualitative tests.
topics.name: Alkenes*: E1 and E2 reactions, kinetics, order of reactivity of alkyl halides, rearrangement of carbocations, Saytzeffs orientation, free radical addition of HBr (anti-Markovnikov's), qualitative tests.

topics.topicName: Conjugated dienes*: Electrophilic addition, 1,2 and 1,4-addition, Diels-Alder reaction.

topics.topicName: Alkyl halides*: SN1 and SN2 reactions - kinetics, mechanism, reactivity, stereochemistry, factors affecting nucleophilic substitutions.
topics.name: Alkyl halides*: SN1 and SN2 reactions - kinetics, mechanism, reactivity, stereochemistry, factors affecting nucleophilic substitutions.

topics.topicName: Alcohols*: Qualitative tests, structure and uses of Ethyl alcohol, Methyl alcohol, Chlorobutanol, Cetostearyl alcohol, Benzyl alcohol, Glycerol, Propylene glycol.
topics.name: Alcohols*: Qualitative tests, structure and uses of Ethyl alcohol, Methyl alcohol, Chlorobutanol, Cetostearyl alcohol, Benzyl alcohol, Glycerol, Propylene glycol.

topics.topicName: Nucleophilic addition, Electromeric effect, Aldol condensation, Cannizzaro's reaction, Claisen-Schmidt condensation, Benzoin condensation.
topics.name: Nucleophilic addition, Electromeric effect, Aldol condensation, Cannizzaro's reaction, Claisen-Schmidt condensation, Benzoin condensation.

topics.topicName: Qualitative tests, structure and uses of Formaldehyde, Paraldehyde, Acetone, Chloral hydrate, Hexamine, Benzaldehyde, Vanillin, Cinnamaldehyde.
topics.name: Qualitative tests, structure and uses of Formaldehyde, Paraldehyde, Acetone, Chloral hydrate, Hexamine, Benzaldehyde, Vanillin, Cinnamaldehyde.

topics.topicName: Carboxylic acids*: Acidity, effect of substituents on acidity, structure and uses of Formic acid, Acetic acid, Oxalic acid, Succinic acid, Citric acid, Salicylic acid, Benzoic acid, Lactic acid, Tartaric acid.
topics.name: Carboxylic acids*: Acidity, effect of substituents on acidity, structure and uses of Formic acid, Acetic acid, Oxalic acid, Succinic acid, Citric acid, Salicylic acid, Benzoic acid, Lactic acid, Tartaric acid.

topics.topicName: Aliphatic amines*: Basicity, effect of substituents on basicity, qualitative tests, structure and uses of Methyl amine, Ethyl amine, Dimethyl amine, Triethyl amine.
topics.name: Aliphatic amines*: Basicity, effect of substituents on basicity, qualitative tests, structure and uses of Methyl amine, Ethyl amine, Dimethyl amine, Triethyl amine.

scope: Biochemistry deals with complete understanding of the chemical processes associated with living cells. The clinical chemistry part of the syllabus has been designed to provide basic concept of clinical tests in kidney, liver and pancreas.
objectives: Understand the catalytic role of enzymes, importance of enzyme inhibitors in design of new drugs, therapeutic and diagnostic applications of enzymes
Understand the metabolic pathways of biomolecules in physiological and pathological states
Understand the genetic organization of mammalian genome and hetero & autocatalytic functions of DNA
courseOutcomes: CO1: Understand and explain bioenergetics, thermodynamics, and energy-rich compounds.
CO2: Describe carbohydrate metabolism, glycolysis, TCA cycle, glycogenolysis, and biological oxidation.
CO3: Explain lipid metabolism, beta-oxidation, de novo synthesis, and amino acid catabolism/transamination.
CO4: Discuss nucleic acid metabolism, DNA replication, transcription, translation, and genetic transcription.
CO5: Detail enzyme kinetics, Michaelis-Menten equation, enzyme inhibition, coenzymes, and clinical markers.

topics.topicName: Introduction, classification, chemical nature and biological role of Carbohydrates, Lipids, Nucleic acids, Amino acids and Proteins.
topics.name: Introduction, classification, chemical nature and biological role of Carbohydrates, Lipids, Nucleic acids, Amino acids and Proteins.

topics.topicName: Bioenergetics: Concept of free energy, endergonic and exergonic reactions, Relationship between free energy, entropy and enthalpy, redox potential.
topics.name: Bioenergetics: Concept of free energy, endergonic and exergonic reactions, Relationship between free energy, entropy and enthalpy, redox potential.

topics.topicName: Energy rich compounds: ATP, cyclic AMP, Creatinine phosphate, their production and biological significance.
topics.name: Energy rich compounds: ATP, cyclic AMP, Creatinine phosphate, their production and biological significance.

topics.name: Carbohydrate metabolism: Glycolysis, TCA cycle, HMP shunt, Glycogenolysis, Glycogenesis, Gluconeogenesis, Hormonal regulation of carbohydrate metabolism, Diabetes mellitus.

topics.name: Biological oxidation: Electron transport chain (ETC) and its mechanism, oxidative phosphorylation, inhibitors of ETC, oxidative phosphorylation.

topics.topicName: Lipid metabolism: Beta-oxidation of saturated fatty acids, de novo synthesis of fatty acids, metabolism of cholesterol, ketoacidosis, atherosclerosis, hypercholesterolemia.
topics.name: Lipid metabolism: Beta-oxidation of saturated fatty acids, de novo synthesis of fatty acids, metabolism of cholesterol, ketoacidosis, atherosclerosis, hypercholesterolemia.

topics.topicName: Amino acid metabolism: Transamination, deamination, decarboxylation, urea cycle, metabolism of phenylalanine and tyrosine, genetic disorders of amino acid metabolism.
topics.name: Amino acid metabolism: Transamination, deamination, decarboxylation, urea cycle, metabolism of phenylalanine and tyrosine, genetic disorders of amino acid metabolism.

topics.name: Biosynthesis of purine and pyrimidine nucleotides, catabolism of purine nucleotides, gout, hyperuricemia.

topics.topicName: Genetic information transfer: DNA replication, transcription, translation, genetic code, regulation of gene expression, mutation and DNA repair.

topics.topicName: Classification, properties, mechanism of enzyme action, Michaelis-Menten equation, enzyme kinetics, factors affecting enzyme activity.

scope: Pathophysiology is the study of the causes of diseases and the reactions of the body to such disease producing agents.
objectives: Describe the etiology and pathogenesis of the selected disease states
Name the signs and symptoms of the diseases
Comprehend the complications of diseases
courseOutcomes: CO1: Discuss cellular adaptation, reversible and irreversible cell injury, pathogenesis of inflammation and repair.
CO2: Describe etiology, pathogenesis, and clinical features of cardiovascular, respiratory, and renal diseases.
CO3: Explain pathophysiology of haematological, endocrine, nervous, and gastrointestinal disorders.
CO4: Discuss inflammatory joint diseases, osteoporosis, gout, and the basic principles of cancer.
CO5: Describe pathogenesis and transmission of major infectious and sexually transmitted diseases.

topics.topicName: Basic principles of Cell injury and Adaptation: Causes of cell injury, pathogenesis, morphology of cell injury, cellular adaptations (atrophy, hypertrophy, hyperplasia, metaplasia, dysplasia).
topics.name: Basic principles of Cell injury and Adaptation: Causes of cell injury, pathogenesis, morphology of cell injury, cellular adaptations (atrophy, hypertrophy, hyperplasia, metaplasia, dysplasia).

topics.topicName: Basic mechanism involved in the process of inflammation and repair: Vascular and cellular events of acute inflammation, chemical mediators, chronic inflammation, wound healing (primary and secondary intention).
topics.name: Basic mechanism involved in the process of inflammation and repair: Vascular and cellular events of acute inflammation, chemical mediators, chronic inflammation, wound healing (primary and secondary intention).

topics.name: Cardiovascular System: Hypertension, congestive heart failure, ischemic heart disease (angina, myocardial infarction, atherosclerosis).

scope: This subject deals with the introduction Database, Database Management System, computer application in clinical studies and use of databases.
objectives: Know the various types of application of computers in pharmacy
Know the various types of databases
Know the various applications of databases in pharmacy
courseOutcomes: CO1: Discuss number systems, binary arithmetic, and concepts of information systems and software.
CO2: Explain web technologies, HTML, XML, CSS, and search engine optimization concepts.
CO3: Describe databases, MS Access, SQL queries, and the application of computers in pharmacy.
CO4: Discuss bioinformatics, databases like BLAST, FASTA, and computer-aided drug design.
CO5: Explain computers in clinical trials, LIMS, and data analysis in preclinical development.

topics.topicName: Number system: Binary, octal, decimal, hexadecimal number systems, conversions, binary addition, subtraction.
topics.name: Number system: Binary, octal, decimal, hexadecimal number systems, conversions, binary addition, subtraction.

topics.topicName: Web design: Introduction to HTML tags, creation of simple web pages using HTML, XML, CSS.

topics.topicName: Pharmacy Applications: Computer applications in pharmacy, inventory control, drug information storage and retrieval, retail pharmacy management.

topics.name: Introduction to Bioinformatics: Biological databases, primary, secondary, composite databases, BLAST, FASTA.

topics.topicName: Applications: Sequence alignment, phylogenetic analysis, protein structure prediction, computer-aided drug design.
topics.name: Applications: Sequence alignment, phylogenetic analysis, protein structure prediction, computer-aided drug design.

topics.topicName: Clinical trials: Application of computers in clinical trials, data management, electronic data capture, CRF, database lock.
topics.name: Clinical trials: Application of computers in clinical trials, data management, electronic data capture, CRF, database lock.

topics.topicName: LIMS & Preclinical: Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS), computers in preclinical development, data analysis, statistical software.

scope: Environmental Sciences is the multidisciplinary science that teaches students the broad perspective of the environment, natural resources, ecosystems, and environmental pollution.
objectives: Create an awareness on environmental problems
Develop an analytical attitude towards environmental issues
Understand natural resources and ecosystems
Understand the impact of environmental pollution
Appreciate environmental policies and ethics
courseOutcomes: CO1: Discuss the multidisciplinary nature of environmental studies and the conservation of natural resources.
CO2: Describe structural components, functions, and energy flow within different types of ecosystems.
CO3: Explain types, causes, effects, and control measures of air, water, soil, and noise pollution.
CO4: Discuss solid waste management, disaster management, and urban environmental problems.
CO5: Explain social issues, environmental protection acts, and the human population's impact on the environment.

topics.topicName: Multidisciplinary nature of environmental studies: Definition, scope and importance; Need for public awareness.
topics.name: Multidisciplinary nature of environmental studies: Definition, scope and importance; Need for public awareness.

topics.name: Natural Resources: Renewable and non-renewable resources, Natural resources and associated problems: Forest resources, Water resources, Mineral resources, Food resources, Energy resources, Land resources.

topics.topicName: Concept of an ecosystem; Structure and function of an ecosystem; Producers, consumers and decomposers; Energy flow in the ecosystem; Ecological succession; Food chains, food webs and ecological pyramids.

courseName: Human Anatomy and Physiology II - Practical
scope: Practical anatomy and physiology is designed to provide hands-on experience in histological study, neurological examination, physiological parameters, and feedback loop mechanisms.
objectives: Understand neurological testing, feedback systems, and slide inspection
Measure respiratory volumes, basal mass index, and total blood count
courseOutcomes: CO1: Study the integumentary, special senses, nervous, and endocrine systems using models and charts.
CO2: Demonstrate general neurological examination, reflex activity, and positive/negative feedback mechanisms.
CO3: Measure physiological parameters such as body temperature, tidal volume, vital capacity, and basal mass index.
CO4: Analyze permanent slides of vital organs and reproductive systems.
CO5: Perform total blood count using cell analyzer and study family planning devices.

scope: This course deals with qualitative organic analysis, solid derivative preparation, and structural construction of molecular models.
objectives: Perform qualitative analysis of unknown organic compounds
Synthesize solid derivatives and assemble molecular models
courseOutcomes: CO1: Perform qualitative analysis of unknown organic compounds containing different functional groups.
CO2: Execute solubility, element detection, and functional group testing.
CO3: Determine melting and boiling points of unknown compounds.
CO4: Synthesize suitable solid derivatives from organic compounds.
CO5: Construct molecular models for understanding organic stereochemistry.

scope: This course introduces biochemical tests, carbohydrate/protein analysis, enzymatic hydrolysis, and clinical diagnostics of blood and urine samples.
objectives: Analyze carbohydrates and proteins qualitatively
Perform quantitative blood and urine chemistry tests
courseOutcomes: CO1: Perform qualitative analysis of carbohydrates and proteins using specific identification tests.
CO2: Conduct quantitative analysis of reducing sugars and proteins.
CO3: Perform qualitative analysis of abnormal constituents of urine.
CO4: Determine blood creatinine, blood sugar, and serum total cholesterol levels.
CO5: Prepare buffer solutions and study salivary amylase activity including enzyme kinetics.

scope: Hands-on sessions focusing on questionnaires, HTML web pages, MS Access database design, SQL, drug information search, and invoice creation.

Apply database concepts to retrieve and present drug information
